mechanism_thesis: context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index) HURTS on these branches: the extra context dimension inflates the coverage map so naive_ctx hoards context-redundant corpus entries and spreads its mutation budget thin, while naive's flat per-edge map stays focused and resolves. naive_ctx BLOCKS, naive RESOLVES.

hypotheses.label: ctx inflates the corpus / map so naive_ctx hoards far more (context-redundant) entries than naive

params.note: count of saved corpus entries per trial; context-redundant hoarding => naive_ctx >> naive

hypotheses.prediction: corpus_count_ratio >= 1.5 (naive_ctx hoards >=1.5x the corpus naive keeps, because the same edges count as new under each call-context). A NON-ctx penalty (e.g. naive simply got luckier on byte mutation) would predict corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0 -- no systematic corpus bloat on the blocking arm.
hypotheses.scope: corpus_queue exists ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (data_realities). Of the 6 members, ONLY harfbuzz_5802 and harfbuzz_6124_h1 are scorable on s4; lcms/libpng/libxml2 are corpus targets on sB (the owning server arbitrates them).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0; rules out 'naive just won by chance' alternative
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures corpus SIZE (the inflation mechanism), independent of the resolve pattern (which gate side each fuzzer takes) that defined the shape

hypotheses.label: a high-call-count / multi-context path at the gate is what ctx fragments (flooding) vs a shallow exact-literal gate (dilution)

params.source: per-role coverage call counts at the blocker function (e.g. png_read_IDAT_data 2750@L vs 136@W)

hypotheses.prediction: FLOODING members (deep high-iteration loop, e.g. libpng inflate loop) show call_count_lift >> 1 (naive_ctx re-enters the loop function far more, manufacturing the (ctx,edge) flood); DILUTION members (shallow 1-hop exact-literal gate, e.g. harfbuzz_6124) show call_count_lift ~ 1 -- the gate is shallow, the penalty is pure signal-splitting not loop flooding. An 'all members are the same flooding mechanism' alternative would predict call_count_lift >> 1 uniformly.
hypotheses.scope: needs per-role / coverage call-count data at the blocker function for both arms; the cards expose this for libpng (2750 vs 136) and harfbuzz, partial elsewhere.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out uniform; separates flooding from dilution
hypotheses.g2_independent: call-count at the gate function is a structural property of the path, not the resolve pattern

hypotheses.label: at a SHALLOW exact-literal equality gate (not a loop, not a hoard), naive's corpus is enriched in the gate's target byte while naive_ctx's corpus is DEPLETED of it -- the per-edge reward that should pull mutation onto the winning literal is split across call-context slots so naive_ctx never assembles it

params.arms: naive_ctx (blocking) vs naive (resolving) -- NOT the registry tool's cmplog-vs-naive; a new arm pairing on the SAME byte_freq_ratio compute
params.signed_target_enrich: log2(target_frac[naive_ctx] / target_frac[naive])  -- fraction of each fuzzer's saved corpus whose byte at the gate offset == the resolving (target) value
params.decoy_enrich: log2(decoy_frac[naive_ctx] / decoy_frac[naive])  -- fraction whose byte == the blocking (decoy) value
params.gate_offsets: libxml2_6605: name[1] offset, target byte 0x6d ('m'/'M'), decoy 0x5f ('_'); harfbuzz_5802: the 0x05E9 SHIN low byte 0xe9 (paired with 0x05C2 SIN-DOT 0xc2 05), decoy = SIN-DOT-only fills (0x00/0xb9/0xc2)
params.note: operand offsets + target/decoy values come from the signature.operand_literal + card byte_diff, exactly as the registry operand_enrichment tool sources them

hypotheses.prediction: DILUTION signature: signed_target_enrich <= -0.5 (naive_ctx corpus DEPLETED of the gate target byte relative to naive -- ctx never assembled the winning literal) AND decoy_enrich >= 0 (naive_ctx pinned on the non-target byte). The obvious alternative -- 'no systematic ctx penalty, naive simply won by luck on these two gates' -- predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (both corpora equally likely to carry the target byte). A pure flooding/hoarding alternative is ALSO ruled out here: these are shallow 1-hop equality gates (depth_reach call_count_lift ~ 1, corpus_size_ratio < 1.5 already observed), so the only ctx-anti signature available is corpus byte-CONTENT depletion at the gate offset, not corpus SIZE or call-depth.
hypotheses.scope: fixed-offset exact-literal value gates only (byte_freq_ratio domain). harfbuzz_5802 has an on-disk corpus on s4 (scorable). libxml2_6605 has NO corpus on s4 but libxml2 is an sB corpus target (cross-server rule: design the test valid for the branch; the owning server arbitrates -- do NOT mark decidable:false for s4 corpus absence). Needs >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (the reps have them).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (no depletion) -> refutes dilution; the prediction is directional and could fall the other way
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures corpus byte CONTENT at the gate offset (the dilution mechanism's footprint) -- a DIFFERENT signal than corpus SIZE (corpus_size_ratio, already tried) or call-DEPTH (depth_reach, blind to context-reach here). It does NOT re-use the naive_ctx-blocks resolve pattern that defined the shape; it asks an independent question (does naive_ctx's corpus carry the literal byte at all).

hypotheses.label: at the shallow exact-literal gate, naive's corpus contains a sliding window that lands CLOSE to the gate operand (low Hamming distance, offset-tolerant) while naive_ctx's best window stalls FAR from it -- ctx's split per-edge reward never pulls any saved entry toward the operand, even when the byte can sit at a drifting/multibyte offset that the fixed-offset depletion test missed

params.arms: naive (resolving winner) vs naive_ctx (blocking loser) -- a NEW arm pairing on the SAME value_distance_reached compute (the registry tool's hardcoded arms are vp/vpc/cmp/naive; here the discriminating pair is naive vs naive_ctx)
params.naive_min_distance: MIN over naive's saved/blocking seeds of (MIN over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width)
params.ctx_min_distance: same min over naive_ctx's saved seeds
params.target_operand: libxml2_6605: 0x6d ('m'/'M' at name[1], width 1); harfbuzz_5802: the 0x05E9 SHIN codepoint (LE bytes e9 05) paired adjacency with 0x05C2 (c2 05), width per signature.operand_width_bytes=3
params.note: offset-TOLERANT (slides every window) -- unlike byte_freq_ratio which pins a single gate offset; this is exactly the handle that the fixed-offset depletion test could not apply to harfbuzz_5802's drifting paired-codepoint operand

hypotheses.prediction: naive's corpus reaches a window with LOW Hamming distance to the operand while naive_ctx's stalls FAR: winner_closer = true AND distance_gap >= 0.15 (naive lands >=0.15 of a width closer to the literal than naive_ctx ever does). This is GENUINELY DIFFERENT from the three refuted priors: (1) NOT corpus SIZE -- corpus_size_ratio < 1.5 already observed; (2) NOT call-DEPTH -- call_count_lift ~ 1 (shallow gate); (3) NOT fixed-offset byte presence -- the depletion test (signed_target_enrich) FAILED to hold for these two because it pins ONE offset, missing a window that carries the operand at a drifted/multibyte position; value_distance_reached slides every window so it CAN see a near-miss that depletion declared absent. The alternative it rules out: 'naive won by luck, both corpora are equidistant from the operand' predicts winner_closer = false / distance_gap ~ 0 (no systematic approach advantage). A second alternative -- 'naive_ctx actually gets just as close but the gate needs an EXACT 0-distance hit naive happened to land' -- predicts distance_gap ~ 0 with naive_min_distance ~ 0; rejected because the rule requires a sustained GAP, not just a lucky exact naive hit.
hypotheses.scope: fixed-or-drifting exact-literal value gates (value_distance_reached domain; short operands width<=2 are chance-alignment prone -- libxml2_6605 is width-1 so flag the chance-alignment caveat; harfbuzz_5802 is width-3, safer). Reads on-disk seeds: harfbuzz_5802 scorable on s4 (corpus target); libxml2_6605 has NO s4 corpus but libxml2 is an sB corpus target -- the owning server arbitrates (cross-server rule: NOT decidable:false for s4 absence).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: directional; could come out winner_closer=false / distance_gap ~ 0, refuting the approach-advantage thesis (naive won by luck). Rules out the equidistant-corpora alternative.
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures how CLOSE each arm's corpus gets to the operand via offset-tolerant sliding-window Hamming distance -- a signal independent of the resolve pattern (which side each fuzzer takes) AND distinct from all three refuted tests (size / call-depth / fixed-offset byte presence). It asks: did naive_ctx's split reward ever pull a saved entry toward the literal at all, anywhere in the window?

hypotheses.label: the residual shallow exact-literal DILUTION sub-mechanism (naive_ctx splits the per-edge reward at a shallow 1-hop equality gate so the winning literal is never assembled) is NOT discriminable by ANY built corpus measurement -- ctx is a non-local FEEDBACK whose footprint these two corpora do not preserve

params.exhausted_handles: All FOUR corpus/seed handles the data_realities permit for a non-local ctx feedback have now been measured on these two branches and NONE held: (1) corpus SIZE (corpus_size_ratio) -- ratio < 1.5 (no hoarding at a shallow gate); (2) call-DEPTH (depth_reach) -- call_count_lift ~ 1 (1-hop gate, no loop flooding); (3) FIXED-offset byte presence (byte_freq_ratio / ctx_literal_corpus_depletion) -- scored, signed_target_enrich did not reach <= -0.5 (the depletion signature is absent); (4) OFFSET-TOLERANT window distance (value_distance_reached / ctx_literal_window_approach) -- scored in round 2, winner_closer AND distance_gap>=0.15 did not hold (no sustained approach gap).
params.untried_tools_assessed_unfit: The two BUILT tools not yet used on this shape -- joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) and token_count -- DO NOT FIT these branches. Both measure structural-token ASSEMBLY for vpc-vs-cmplog/vp joint-necessity arms; these gates are a single-byte ('m'/'M') and a paired-codepoint (SHIN+SIN-DOT) EQUALITY, with the decisive pair naive vs naive_ctx and NO structural-assembly story. Applying them would force a mechanism the gate does not carry (violates G1 discrimination + G3 honesty). Therefore no untried built measurement rescues these branches.

hypotheses.prediction: NONE -- decidable:false. Every measurement the data_realities allow for a non-local ctx feedback (corpus size / call-depth / fixed-offset byte content / offset-tolerant window distance) has been scored and refuted for this pair, and the remaining built tools (joint_necessity, token_count) measure a structural-assembly mechanism these shallow equality gates do not exhibit. There is no built corpus measurement whose result would distinguish 'ctx split-reward dilution' from 'naive won by chance on a shallow gate'. An honest inconclusive is the correct outcome.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -- no rule; these two branches fall to fallback:inconclusive
hypotheses.scope: applies to the two residual shallow exact-literal equality gates (libxml2_6605 width-1 name[1]=='m'/'M'; harfbuzz_5802 paired-codepoint SHIN+SIN-DOT) after all four corpus handles were exhausted across rounds 1-2.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: n/a (decidable:false). This is NOT a forced label -- it is the documented exhaustion of every discriminating measurement the data permits, which G3 marks as a CORRECT outcome for a non-local feedback family.
hypotheses.g2_independent: n/a -- no signal is asserted; the branches are left unlabeled (inconclusive), preserving label-source/verification independence by declining to relabel without evidence.

decidable_caveat: PARTIALLY decidable, and now CLOSED (round-3 final). The 4 CONFIRMED branches re-validate under the KEPT priors (ctx_corpus_inflation flooding/hoarding + ctx_depth_inflation_at_gate). The 2 UNLABELED branches (libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802) are the SHALLOW exact-literal DILUTION sub-mechanism and are now honestly DECIDABLE:FALSE. Across rounds 1-2 all FOUR corpus/seed handles permitted for a non-local ctx feedback were scored on this pair and NONE held: corpus SIZE (ratio<1.5), call-DEPTH (call_count_lift~1), FIXED-offset byte presence (byte_freq_ratio depletion did not reach <=-0.5), and OFFSET-TOLERANT window distance (value_distance_reached winner_closer/distance_gap did not hold -- the round-2 contingency triggered). The two remaining BUILT tools (joint_necessity, token_count) measure structural-token ASSEMBLY for vpc-vs-cmplog joint-necessity and do NOT fit a single-byte / paired-codepoint EQUALITY gate with a naive-vs-naive_ctx decisive pair -- forcing them would invent a mechanism (G3 violation). With every permitted discriminating measurement exhausted, the shallow-literal ctx-dilution mechanism is NON-DISCRIMINABLE by any built corpus measurement; these two branches correctly fall to fallback:inconclusive. This is the legitimate non-local-feedback outcome G3 anticipates, NOT a decidability failure of design and NOT a cross-server boundary (the round-2 window_approach test WAS scored and refuted, so this is exhaustion, not absence). CONFIRMED branches stay decidable:true; the residual hypothesis ctx_shallow_literal_dilution_nonlocal is the explicit decidable:false carrier for the 2 unlabeled branches.
data_realities_respected: NO seed_lineage 'ctx event' measurement -- ctx, like value_profile, is a FEEDBACK invisible in mutation_op; the only handles are what the corpus REACHES/HOARDS (size, call-depth) or CARRIES (byte content / window distance at the operand), never a lineage event. All four such handles are now exhausted on the 2 unlabeled branches.
corpus_queue / byte_freq_ratio / value_distance_reached scored only on corpus targets (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4; lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty on sB).

byte_freq_ratio (depletion) and value_distance_reached (window_approach) apply ONLY to the two fixed/drifting EXACT-LITERAL gates (libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802); NOT to the length/structural/loop members (lcms length-underflow, libpng deep-loop) -- those keep the size/depth tests, honoring the rule that byte-alignment fails on structural/length gates.

joint_necessity / token_count (struct_size_and_token_count) measure structural-ASSEMBLY for vpc-vs-cmplog/vp joint-necessity arms -- they do NOT apply to a naive-vs-naive_ctx single-byte/paired-codepoint EQUALITY gate; not proposed for these branches (would force an assembly mechanism the gate lacks).
value_distance_reached short-operand caveat: libxml2_6605 is width-1 (chance-alignment prone -- flagged); harfbuzz_5802 is width-3 (safer). Both scored in round 2; neither held.

cross-server: the two unlabeled branches' decidable:false verdict is an EXHAUSTION-of-discriminating-measurements outcome (every permitted handle scored and refuted), NOT an s4 corpus-absence artifact -- the round-2 window_approach test was scored for both and did not hold, so the owning server has already arbitrated.
notes: ROUND-3 (FINAL, loop-until-dry). KEPT all FOUR priors VERBATIM (ctx_corpus_inflation, ctx_depth_inflation_at_gate, ctx_literal_corpus_depletion, ctx_literal_window_approach) so the confirmed branches re-validate and the superset/monotonic invariant holds. The 2 UNLABELED branches (libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802) remain rule_not_met: round-2's ctx_literal_window_approach (the offset-tolerant value_distance_reached handle) was scored and did NOT hold -- exactly the round-2 documented contingency. That exhausts all four corpus/seed handles the data_realities permit for a non-local ctx FEEDBACK: corpus SIZE (ratio<1.5), call-DEPTH (call_count_lift~1), FIXED-offset byte presence (byte_freq_ratio depletion absent), OFFSET-TOLERANT window distance (value_distance_reached gap absent). The only BUILT tools never tried here -- joint_necessity and token_count -- measure structural-token ASSEMBLY for the vpc-vs-cmplog joint-necessity subtype; they do NOT fit a single-byte ('m'/'M', libxml2_6605) or paired-codepoint (SHIN+SIN-DOT, harfbuzz_5802) EQUALITY gate whose decisive pair is naive vs naive_ctx with no assembly story. Forcing them would invent a mechanism (G3 violation) and fail G1 discrimination. THEREFORE: I add NO new measured hypothesis. Instead I add ONE residual hypothesis, ctx_shallow_literal_dilution_nonlocal, marked decidable:false, that explicitly carries the 2 unlabeled branches to fallback:inconclusive and documents the exhausted-handles + unfit-tools reasoning. This is the legitimate, CORRECT outcome G3 anticipates for a non-local ctx feedback whose footprint the corpus does not preserve at a shallow exact-literal gate -- an honest inconclusive, not a forced label. PRIORS KEPT: ctx_corpus_inflation, ctx_depth_inflation_at_gate, ctx_literal_corpus_depletion, ctx_literal_window_approach (all 4, verbatim). NEW: ctx_shallow_literal_dilution_nonlocal (decidable:false). DECIDABLE:FALSE SUB-GROUP: libxml2_6605, harfbuzz_5802 (the shallow exact-literal dilution pair). NO new discriminating measurement proposed -- every permitted handle is exhausted and the two untried tools are mechanism-unfit.
